title: Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction
description: The definitive textbook on reinforcement learning by Sutton and Barto, a clear, intuitive development of the core ideas, from multi-armed bandits and Markov decision processes to temporal-difference learning, function approximation, and policy gradients. Free to read.
longDescription: Widely regarded as the bible of reinforcement learning, this book by Richard Sutton and Andrew Barto gives a clear and accessible account of the field's key ideas and algorithms. Starting from multi-armed bandits and finite Markov decision processes, it builds up dynamic programming, Monte Carlo methods, and temporal-difference learning, then extends to function approximation, eligibility traces, policy-gradient methods, and the connections between RL, psychology, and neuroscience. The second edition is freely available online and remains the standard reference for anyone learning RL.

title: Mathematics for Machine Learning
description: A self-contained guide to the mathematics underpinning machine learning: linear algebra, analytic geometry, calculus, probability, and optimization, connected directly to core ML methods. Free to read online.
longDescription: Mathematics for Machine Learning builds the mathematical foundations needed to understand modern machine learning, assuming little beyond high-school math. The first part develops linear algebra, analytic geometry, matrix decompositions, vector calculus, probability and distributions, and continuous optimization. The second part shows these tools in action across four central ML methods: linear regression, principal component analysis, Gaussian mixture models, and support vector machines. It is an ideal bridge for readers who want to follow ML research and textbooks with confidence, and is freely available online.

title: An Introduction to Statistical Learning
description: The most accessible introduction to statistical learning, covering regression, classification, resampling, regularization, trees, SVMs, and deep learning, with intuitive explanations and hands-on labs. Free, with R and Python editions.
longDescription: An Introduction to Statistical Learning (ISL) presents the key ideas of modern statistical machine learning in an accessible, application-oriented way, with far less mathematical prerequisite than its advanced sibling, The Elements of Statistical Learning. It covers linear and logistic regression, resampling methods (cross-validation and the bootstrap), regularization, tree-based methods and ensembles, support vector machines, unsupervised learning, and a chapter on deep learning, each paired with hands-on labs. Available free as a PDF in both R (ISLR) and Python (ISLP) editions, it is one of the most widely used ML texts in the world.

title: Probabilistic Machine Learning
description: Kevin Murphy's modern, comprehensive reference on machine learning from a probabilistic perspective, in two free volumes spanning foundations (Introduction) and advanced methods (Advanced Topics).
longDescription: Probabilistic Machine Learning is Kevin Murphy's sweeping, up-to-date treatment of machine learning grounded in probability. Book 1, 'An Introduction' (2022), covers the foundations: probability, linear and logistic regression, deep neural networks, and core supervised and unsupervised methods. Book 2, 'Advanced Topics' (2023), goes deeper into generative models, Bayesian inference, graphical models, reinforcement learning, and more. Together they form one of the most comprehensive modern references in the field, with both volumes freely available online alongside accompanying code.

title: Build a Reasoning Model (From Scratch)
description: A hands-on guide to how reasoning LLMs actually work, starting from a pre-trained base model and adding reasoning step by step in code, including inference-time scaling, reinforcement learning, and distillation.
longDescription: In Build a Reasoning Model (from Scratch), you learn how a reasoning large language model works by building the capabilities yourself. Rather than describing reasoning only in theory, the book takes a hands-on approach: starting from a pre-trained base LLM, you add reasoning step by step in code so you can see exactly how it works. It focuses on techniques not covered in the author's Build a Large Language Model (from Scratch), including inference-time scaling, reinforcement-learning training, and distillation. The two books complement each other but can also be read independently.

title: Foundations of Computer Vision
description: A modern, intuition-first introduction to computer vision from an image-processing and machine-learning perspective, packed with visualizations. Free to read online.
longDescription: Foundations of Computer Vision covers the field's core topics from an image-processing and machine-learning perspective, with an emphasis on building intuition through many visualizations. Written for undergraduate and graduate students entering the field, though valuable to experienced practitioners too, it spans classical image processing, visual representation and learning, and modern deep-learning approaches to vision. Part of MIT Press's Adaptive Computation and Machine Learning series, a free-to-read online version is available on the book's website.

title: Information Theory, Inference, and Learning Algorithms
description: David MacKay's classic that unifies information theory and machine learning, covering data compression, error-correcting codes, Bayesian inference, and neural networks, full of intuition and exercises. Free to read online.
longDescription: A landmark textbook by David J.C. MacKay that brings information theory and machine learning together in a single, intuitive narrative. It covers data compression and source coding, noisy-channel coding and error-correcting codes, probability and Bayesian inference, Monte Carlo methods, and neural networks, emphasizing the deep connections between them. Known for its engaging style, vivid examples, and abundant exercises, the book has introduced generations of students to probabilistic thinking. The full text is freely available to read online.

title: Deep Learning: Foundations and Concepts
description: A modern, self-contained introduction to deep learning by Christopher and Hugh Bishop, building from foundational concepts to the architectures behind today's models, with clear explanations and the essential mathematics. Free to read online.
longDescription: Deep Learning: Foundations and Concepts offers a comprehensive, up-to-date introduction to the ideas underpinning modern deep learning. Written by Christopher M. Bishop (author of the classic Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning) with Hugh Bishop, it develops the subject from first principles (probability, neural networks, and backpropagation) through to modern topics such as transformers, diffusion models, and graph neural networks. The book balances intuition with the necessary mathematics, making it accessible to newcomers while remaining valuable to practitioners. A free-to-use online version is available on the book's website.

title: AI Research Experiences (Harvard CS197)
description: Harvard's CS197 as a written course: a practical guide to leveling up as an AI researcher, covering modern tooling (PyTorch, Lightning, Hugging Face, Weights & Biases, AWS/Colab) plus how to read papers, generate ideas, write, present, and run research projects.
longDescription: Take your AI skills to the next level. This written course covers modern development tools like PyTorch, Lightning, and Hugging Face, and streamlines your workflow with VSCode, Git, and Conda. You'll learn to use the cloud with AWS and Colab to train large deep learning models with fast GPU acceleration, and to manage many experiments with Weights & Biases. Beyond tooling, it teaches how to systematically read research papers, generate new ideas, and present them in slides or papers, along with the project management and team communication techniques used by top AI researchers. Taught by Professor Pranav Rajpurkar; the full text is available as an online book.

title: How to Scale Your Model: A Systems View of LLMs on TPUs
description: A free online book from Google DeepMind that demystifies the science of scaling language models: how TPUs and GPUs work and communicate, how LLMs run on real hardware, and how to parallelize training and inference to run efficiently at massive scale.
longDescription: Training LLMs often feels like alchemy, but understanding and optimizing their performance doesn't have to. This book by Google DeepMind demystifies the science of scaling language models: how TPUs (and GPUs) work and how they communicate with each other, how LLMs run on real hardware, and how to parallelize models during training and inference so they run efficiently at massive scale. If you've ever wondered how expensive an LLM should be to train, how much memory you need to serve a model yourself, or what an 'AllGather' actually is, this is for you.

title: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
description: A concise, accessible introduction to RLHF and post-training for language models, covering preference data, reward modeling, policy optimization, and direct alignment methods. Free to read online, with a companion video course.
longDescription: Nathan Lambert's RLHF Book is a short, focused introduction to reinforcement learning from human feedback and modern post-training of language models. It walks through the full pipeline (preference data, reward modeling, policy-gradient and PPO-style optimization, rejection sampling, and direct alignment algorithms such as DPO) with an emphasis on what actually matters in practice. The book is free to read online and is accompanied by a companion video lecture series / post-training course.

title: Linear Algebra Done Right
description: A celebrated introduction to linear algebra that develops the subject through linear maps rather than determinants, emphasizing understanding over computation. The acclaimed 4th edition is freely available online.
longDescription: Sheldon Axler's classic takes a distinctive approach to undergraduate linear algebra: it builds the theory of vector spaces and linear operators without relying on determinants until the very end, leading to cleaner, more conceptual proofs. Topics include vector spaces, eigenvalues and eigenvectors, inner product spaces, the spectral theorem, and operators on real and complex vector spaces. The fourth edition is published open access by Springer under a Creative Commons license and can be read freely online, making it an excellent foundation for the math behind machine learning.

title: The Art of Doing Science and Engineering: Learning to Learn
description: Richard Hamming's classic on the practice of science and engineering, and on learning how to learn. Drawn from his famous Naval Postgraduate School course, it blends technical insight with hard-won lessons on doing work that matters.
longDescription: Based on a course Richard Hamming taught at the U.S. Naval Postgraduate School, this book is part technical manual and part career memoir. First published in 1997 and reissued by Stripe Press in 2020, it ranges across digital filters, coding and information theory, simulation, and the nature of great research, alongside reflections on creativity, style, and how to position yourself to do important work. Its central theme is 'learning to learn': cultivating the habits, judgment, and taste that let you keep growing long after the specific facts you know become obsolete. It pairs naturally with Hamming's celebrated talk, 'You and Your Research.'

title: Deep Learning
description: The definitive textbook on deep learning, covering both theoretical foundations and practical applications.
longDescription: Written by three experts in the field, Deep Learning is the only comprehensive book on the subject. It provides much-needed broad perspective and mathematical preliminaries for beginners, explains cutting-edge research, and presents best practices with advice on the practical aspects of implementing deep learning techniques.

title: Understanding Deep Learning
description: An authoritative and accessible introduction to deep learning, balancing theory and practice.
longDescription: This book provides a comprehensive overview of deep learning, covering key topics and recent advances such as transformers and diffusion models. It presents concepts in an intuitive form, with minimal mathematical prerequisites, extensive illustrations, and practice problems. The book is suitable for anyone with a basic background in applied mathematics.

title: Hands-On Machine Learning with Scikit-Learn, Keras, and TensorFlow
description: A practical guide to machine learning with Python and its libraries.
longDescription: Through a series of recent breakthroughs, deep learning has boosted the entire field of machine learning. Now, even programmers who know close to nothing about this technology can use simple, efficient tools to implement programs capable of learning from data. This book might get less attention perhaps due to using TensorFlow but it is one of the best practical books on machine learning in general.

title: Build a Large Language Model from Scratch
description: This book guides readers through creating their own large language model (LLM) from the ground up, covering each stage with clear explanations and code examples.
longDescription: The book provides a step-by-step approach to understanding how LLMs work by coding them from scratch. It covers topics such as data preparation, architecture design, pretraining, and finetuning. The method mirrors the approach used in creating large-scale foundational models like those behind ChatGPT. The book includes code for loading and finetuning larger pretrained models, making it suitable for both educational purposes and practical applications.

title: Machine Learning with PyTorch and Scikit-Learn
description: This book provides a comprehensive introduction to machine learning, covering both traditional methods and deep learning techniques using PyTorch.
longDescription: The book starts with foundational concepts in machine learning using scikit-learn, including data preprocessing, classification algorithms, and model evaluation. It then transitions to deep learning with PyTorch, covering topics such as neural networks, convolutional neural networks (CNNs), recurrent neural networks (RNNs), transformers, and generative adversarial networks (GANs). The book includes practical code examples and is suitable for readers with basic Python knowledge.

title: The Little Book of Deep Learning
description: A concise introduction to deep learning, covering foundational concepts, model architectures, and practical applications.
longDescription: This book provides a comprehensive yet accessible overview of deep learning, focusing on key topics such as machine learning basics, efficient computation, neural network components, and various architectures like MLPs, CNNs, and transformers. It also explores applications in image processing, speech recognition, and text generation. Designed to be read on a phone screen, it is suitable for both beginners and experienced practitioners seeking a solid understanding of deep learning principles.

title: Dive into Deep Learning
description: This interactive book provides a comprehensive introduction to deep learning, covering both theoretical foundations and practical implementations.
longDescription: The book starts from scratch, explaining deep learning concepts, context, and code. It covers topics such as neural networks, convolutional neural networks (CNNs), recurrent neural networks (RNNs), transformers, and optimization algorithms. The book is implemented with multiple frameworks like PyTorch, TensorFlow, and JAX, offering executable Jupyter notebooks for hands-on learning. It includes discussions on computer vision, natural language processing, and automatic speech recognition, making it suitable for beginners and experienced practitioners alike.

title: Designing Machine Learning Systems
description: This book provides a holistic approach to designing machine learning systems, focusing on reliability, scalability, and maintainability.
longDescription: Chip Huyen's book addresses the complexities of ML systems by examining key components such as data processing, feature engineering, model selection, and deployment strategies. It emphasizes the importance of aligning ML systems with business objectives and offers practical advice on handling challenges like data distribution shifts and model monitoring. The book is enriched with real-world case studies and insights from industry practitioners.

title: Computer Vision: Algorithms and Applications
description: This book provides a comprehensive introduction to computer vision, covering both foundational algorithms and practical applications.
longDescription: Richard Szeliski's book covers a wide range of topics in computer vision, including image formation, feature detection, stereo vision, motion estimation, and object recognition. It emphasizes both theoretical foundations and practical applications, making it suitable for students and practitioners alike. The book includes discussions on recent advances in the field and provides a solid foundation for understanding computer vision techniques.
